creator: wbe@psr.com
text:
My current thinking is that a suitable solution would be to add to freebsd-update something simple like:

$ if stat /boot/kernel/kernel | fgrep -e 'r-xr-xr-x' >/dev/null; then
  echo 'FreeBSD recommends removing execute permission from the files in /boot/kernel/.'
  echo 'Doing so is backward compatible with previous versions.'
  if continuep(); then
    chmod -x /boot/kernel/*
  fi
fi

Someone please confirm that "compatible with previous versions" is correct, or change it to "with version # or later."
